heading: Sexual assault forensic exam program grants
label: 40723 Sexual assault forensic exam program grants
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 14136a of Title 42

Amendments2022—Subsec. (a). , added subsec. (a) and struck out former subsec. (a). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Attorney General shall make grants to eligible entities to provide training, technical assistance, education, equipment, and information relating to the identification, collection, preservation, analysis, and use of DNA samples and DNA evidence by medical personnel and other personnel, including doctors, medical examiners, coroners, nurses, victim service providers, and other professionals involved in treating victims of sexual assault and sexual assault examination programs, including SANE (Sexual Assault Nurse Examiner), SAFE (Sexual Assault Forensic Examiner), and SART (Sexual Assault Response Team).”Pub. L. 117–103, § 1318(b)Subsec. (b). , (c), added subsec. (b) and struck out former subsec. (b) which defined “eligible entity”.Pub. L. 117–103, § 1318(b)Subsec. (c). , (d)(2), added subsec. (c) and struck out former subsec. (c) which related to preference given to certain eligible entities for grants and promoting the role and employment of forensic nurses.Pub. L. 117–103, § 1318(b)Subsec. (d). , added subsec. (d). Former subsec. (d) redesignated (e).Pub. L. 117–103, § 1318(d)(2)Subsec. (e). , (e), redesignated subsec. (d) as (e) and amended it generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. authorized appropriation of $30,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2024 to carry out this section.Pub. L. 117–103, § 1318(d)(2)2019—Subsec. (d). substituted “2019 through 2024” for “2015 through 2019”.Pub. L. 116–1042018—Subsec. (c)(2). inserted “, both adult and pediatric,” after “role of forensic nurses” and substituted “elder abuse, and, in particular, the need for pediatric sexual assault nurse examiners, including such nurse examiners working in the multidisciplinary setting, in responding to abuse of both children and adolescents” for “and elder abuse”.Pub. L. 115–1072016—Subsecs. (c), (d). added subsec. (c) and redesignated former subsec. (c) as (d).Pub. L. 114–3242014—Subsec. (c). substituted “2015 through 2019” for “2009 through 2014”.Pub. L. 113–1822008—Subsec. (c). substituted “2009 through 2014” for “2005 through 2009”.Pub. L. 110–360

children.label: A
children.text: a State, Tribal, or local government or hospital;

children.label: i
children.text: a SANE program;

children.label: ii
children.text: a SAFE program;

children.label: iii
children.text: a SART program;

children.label: iv
children.text: medical personnel, including a doctor or nurse, involved in treating victims of sexual assault; and

children.label: v
children.text: a victim service provider involved in treating victims of sexual assault;

children.label: C
children.text: a State sexual assault coalition;

children.label: D
children.text: a health care facility, including a hospital that provides sexual assault forensic examinations by a qualified or certified SANE or SAFE;

children.label: E
children.text: a sexual assault examination program that provides SANE or SAFE training; and

children.label: F
children.text: a community-based program that provides sexual assault forensic examinations, including pediatric forensic exams in a multidisciplinary setting, by a qualified or certified SANE or SAFE outside of a traditional health care setting.

children.heading: Health care facility
children.label: 2 Health care facility
children.text: The term “health care facility” means any State, local, Tribal, community, free, nonprofit, academic, or private medical facility, including a hospital, that provides emergency medical care to patients.

children.label: A
children.text: gathering information from the patient for the medical forensic history;

children.label: B
children.text: an examination;

children.label: C
children.text: coordinating treatment of injuries, documentation of biological and physical findings, and collection of evidence from the patient;

children.label: D
children.text: documentation of findings;

children.label: E
children.text: providing information, treatment, and referrals for sexually transmitted infections, pregnancy, suicidal ideation, alcohol and substance abuse, and other non-acute medical concerns; and

children.label: F
children.text: providing follow-up as needed to provide additional healing, treatment, or collection of evidence.

children.heading: Pediatric SANE and SAFE
children.label: 4 Pediatric SANE and SAFE
children.text: The term “pediatric SANE and SAFE” means a SANE or SAFE who is trained to conduct sexual assault forensic examinations on children and youth between the ages of 0 and 18.

children.heading: Qualified personnel
children.label: 5 Qualified personnel
children.text: The term “qualified personnel” includes a registered or advanced practice nurse, physician, doctor of osteopathy, or physician assistant who has specialized training conducting medical forensic examinations.

children.heading: Qualified SANE and SAFE training program
children.label: 6 Qualified SANE and SAFE training program
A
children.text: is qualified to prepare current and future sexual assault nurse examiners to be profession-ready and meet the applicable State and National certification and licensure requirements, through didactic, clinical, preceptor, or capstone programs that include longer-term training;

children.label: B
children.text: provides that preparation under a health care model that uses trauma-informed techniques; and

children.label: C
children.text: is approved as meeting the most recent National Training Standards for Sexual Assault Medical Forensic Examiners.

children.heading: Rural area
children.label: 7 Rural area
children.text: The term “rural area” has the meaning given the term in .section 12291 of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 8 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Sexual assault
children.label: 9 Sexual assault
children.text: The term “sexual assault” means any nonconsensual sexual act or sexual contact proscribed by Federal, Tribal, or State law, including when the individual lacks capacity to consent.

children.heading: Sexual assault forensic examiner; SAFE
children.label: 10 Sexual assault forensic examiner; SAFE
children.text: The term “sexual assault forensic examiner” or “SAFE” means an individual who has specialized forensic training in treating sexual assault survivors and conducting medical forensic examinations.

children.label: A
children.text: gathering information from the patient for the medical forensic history;

children.label: B
children.text: an examination;

children.label: C
children.text: coordinating treatment of injuries, documentation of biological and physical findings, and collection of evidence from the patient;

children.label: D
children.text: documentation of findings;

children.label: E
children.text: providing information, treatment, and referrals for sexually transmitted infections, pregnancy, suicidal ideation, alcohol and substance abuse, and other non-acute medical concerns; and

children.label: F
children.text: providing follow-up as needed to provide additional healing, treatment, or collection of evidence.

children.heading: Sexual assault nurse examiner; SANE
children.label: 12 Sexual assault nurse examiner; SANE
children.text: The term “sexual assault nurse examiner” or “SANE” means a registered or advanced practice nurse who has specialized training conducting medical forensic examinations.

children.label: A
children.text: provides a specialized and immediate response to survivors of sexual assault; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include health care personnel, law enforcement representatives, community-based survivor advocates, prosecutors, and forensic scientists.

children.heading: State
children.label: 14 State
children.text: The term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory or possession of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: use a patient-centered approach to providing services or care;

children.label: B
children.text: promote the dignity, strength, and empowerment of patients who have experienced trauma; and

children.label: C
children.text: incorporate evidence-based practices based on knowledge about the impact of trauma on patients’ lives.

children.heading: Underserved populations
children.label: 16 Underserved populations
children.text: The term “underserved populations” has the meaning given the term in .section 12291 of this title

children.heading: Sexual assault nurse examiner training program grants
children.label: b Sexual assault nurse examiner training program grants
i
children.text: to provide clinical education for SANE students;

children.label: ii
children.text: to provide salaries for full and part-time SANE instructors, including those specializing in pediatrics and working in a multidisciplinary team setting, to help with the clinical training of SANEs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to provide access to simulation laboratories and other resources necessary for clinical education.

children.label: B
children.text: To provide full and part time salaries for SANEs and SAFEs, including pediatric SANEs and SAFEs.

children.label: C
children.text: To increase access to SANEs and SAFEs by otherwise providing training, education, or technical assistance relating to the collection, preservation, analysis, and use of DNA samples and DNA evidence by SANEs, SAFEs, and other qualified personnel.

children.label: A
children.text: establish qualified SANE training programs in localities with a high volume of forensic trauma cases, including adult and child sexual assault, domestic violence, elder abuse, sex trafficking, and strangulation cases;

children.label: B
children.text: increase the local and regional availability of full and part time sexual assault nurse examiners in a rural area, Tribal area, an area with a health professional shortage, or for an underserved population, including efforts to provide culturally competent services; or

children.label: C
children.text: establish or sustain sexual assault mobile teams or units or otherwise enhance SANE and SAFE access through telehealth.

children.heading: Directive to the Attorney General
children.label: c Directive to the Attorney General
A
children.text: the availability of grant funding under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the role of sexual assault nurse examiners, both adult and pediatric, and available resources of the Department of Justice and the Department of Health and Human Services to train or employ sexual assault nurses examiners to address the needs of communities dealing with sexual assault, domestic violence, sex trafficking, elder abuse, strangulation, and, in particular, the need for pediatric SANEs, including such nurse examiners working in the multidisciplinary setting, in responding to abuse of both children and adolescents.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 2 Requirement
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the Attorney General shall collaborate with nongovernmental organizations representing SANEs.

children.heading: Public information on access to sexual assault forensic examinations
children.label: d Public information on access to sexual assault forensic examinations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Attorney General, in consultation with the Secretary, shall establish, and update annually, a public website on the access to forensic nurse examiners.March 15, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: funding opportunities for SANE training and continuing education; and

children.label: B
children.text: the availability of sexual assault advocates at locations providing sexual assault forensic exams.

children.label: A
children.text: the availability of, and patient access to, trained SANEs and other providers who perform MFEs or sexual assault forensic examinations;

children.label: B
children.text: the health care facilities, including hospitals or clinics, that offer SANEs and sexual assault forensic examinations and whether each health care facility, including a hospital or clinic, has full-time, part-time, or on-call coverage;

children.label: C
children.text: regional, provider, or other barriers to access for SANE care and services, including MFEs and sexual assault forensic examinations;

children.label: D
children.text: State requirements, minimum standards, and protocols for training SANEs, including trauma-informed and culturally competent training standards;

children.label: E
children.text: State requirements, minimum standards, and protocols for training emergency services personnel involved in MFEs and sexual assault forensic examinations;

children.label: F
children.text: the availability of sexual assault nurse examiner training, frequency of when training is convened, the providers of such training, the State’s role in such training, and what process or procedures are in place for continuing education of such examiners;

children.label: G
children.text: the dedicated Federal and State funding to support SANE training;

children.label: H
children.text: funding opportunities for SANE training and continuing education;

children.label: I
children.text: the availability of sexual assault advocates at locations providing MFEs and sexual assault forensic exams; and

children.label: J
children.text: the total annual cost of conducting sexual assault forensic exams described in .section 10449(b) of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $30,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027 to carry out this section.
